nodes.description: Pole and line fishing remains the most common traditional fishing method employed in the Maldives, particularly for catching skipjack tuna, yellowfin tuna, and bigeye tuna.

nodes.title: [2023-05-29 03:30] Maldives faces challenges in maintaining sustainable fishing practices due to overfishing and illegal fishing

nodes.title: [2023-05-29 03:30] Climate change poses a significant threat to the Maldives' fishing industry

nodes.title: [2023-05-29 03:30] Maldives government invests in research and community outreach to promote sustainable fishing practices
nodes.description: Despite challenges, the Maldives is committed to promoting sustainable fishing practices and ensuring the long-term viability of its fishing industry, investing in research, education, and community outreach programs.

nodes.description: Future NASA deep space missions will encounter communication barriers due to distance, including lag from light distance and intermittent loss of communications.

nodes.description: Due to communication barriers, crews will be required to manage, plan, and execute missions autonomously and respond to unanticipated system failures.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 02:49] Implementing cognitive agents presents challenges in software architecture
nodes.description: Challenges include the need for agents to take responsibility for mission operations while operating under crew directives and coordinating with human operators.

nodes.description: Cognitive agents must adapt to new challenges during long-duration spaceflight, requiring the ability to learn and reconfigure themselves in collaboration with the crew.

nodes.description: Cognitive architectures for space missions must ensure that learned and autonomous behavior is transparent, predictable, and verifiable using traditional software assurance techniques.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 02:49] Cognitive agents should be open and modular for NASA missions

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Jagannath Temple in Puri is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Jagannath, Subhadra, and Balabhadra
nodes.description: The Jagannath Temple in Puri is considered one of the holiest shrines in India and is a major pilgrimage destination for Hindus.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Annual Rath Yatra festival is celebrated in Puri
nodes.description: The annual Rath Yatra festival, also known as the Chariot Festival, is one of the biggest festivals in India and is celebrated in Puri.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Deities of Lord Jagannath, Subhadra, and Balabhadra are taken out of the temple during Rath Yatra festival
nodes.description: During the festival, the deities are taken out of the temple and placed on three huge chariots, which are pulled by thousands of devotees through the streets of Puri.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Rath Yatra festival symbolizes unity and equality among participants
nodes.description: The festival is also a symbol of unity and equality, as people from all castes and creeds come together to participate in the procession.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] User plans to visit Jagannath Temple and take part in Rath Yatra festival celebrations
nodes.description: User expressed a desire to visit the Jagannath Temple someday and take part in the celebrations of the Rath Yatra festival.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Snana Yatra is a ritual before the Rath Yatra festival
nodes.description: Before the Rath Yatra festival, the deities are taken out of the temple and bathed with 108 pots of water in a ritual called the Snana Yatra, which is considered to be a purification ritual for the deities.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Bahuda Yatra is a procession on the ninth day of the Rath Yatra festival
nodes.description: On the ninth day of the Rath Yatra festival, the deities are brought back to the Jagannath Temple in a procession called Bahuda Yatra, which is considered an equally grand procession as the Rath Yatra.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Niladri Bije is a ritual on the last day of the Rath Yatra festival
nodes.description: On the last day of the festival, the deities are taken inside the Jagannath temple in a ritual called Niladri Bije, where they are dressed in new attire and placed on the Ratna Simhasana, the throne of jewels.

nodes.title: [2023-05-30 19:59] Chhera Pahara is an important ritual of the Rath Yatra festival
nodes.description: Chhera Pahara is a ritual where the Gajapati King, who is considered the chief servitor of Lord Jagannath, sweeps the road in front of the chariots with a broom made of gold.
